Tool Call: retrieve("fingers with bilateral dorsal interossei")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the retrieved evidence, there isn't any clear indication suggesting that a certain number of dorsal interossei attach uniquely to another specific finger beyond normal variation noted in individual cases. The studies mention variations like some dorsal interossei being unipennate rather than bipolar, yet do not specify unique bilateral attachments linked definitively to just one named digit out of our given options A-Little B-Index C-Middle D-Ring consistently throughout literature reviewed.
